openmeter/billing/charges/lineage/adapte.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing lineage.Adapter for credit realization lineage and segment persistence. Pure persistence layer: all reads, bulk creates, row-locking FOR UPDATE queries, and close/create segment mutations — no business logic.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method body
patterns.description: Every public method wraps its Ent calls in entutils.TransactingRepo or entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue so operations rebind to any transaction already in ctx rather than using the raw *entdb.Client field.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) error { _, err := tx.db.CreditRealizationLineage.CreateBulk(...).Save(ctx); return err })

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx(ctx) (context.Context, transaction.Driver, error), WithTx(ctx, *TxDriver) *adapter, and Self() *adapter — required by entutils.TransactingRepo to rebind to in-progress transactions.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDB := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDB.Client()} }

patterns.name: GetDriverFromContext guard on lock methods
patterns.description: LockCorrectionLineages and LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill call entutils.GetDriverFromContext and return an error immediately if no active transaction is found — FOR UPDATE locks are only meaningful inside a transaction.
patterns.example: if _, err := entutils.GetDriverFromContext(ctx); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("lock correction lineages must be called in a transaction: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Package-level standalone helper
patterns.description: LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID is exposed as a package-level function (not only a method) so other sub-packages can reuse it without constructing a full adapter through DI; internally it wraps a temporary &adapter{db: db} in TransactingRepo.
patterns.example: func LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID(ctx context.Context, db *entdb.Client, ...) (...) { repo := &adapter{db: db}; return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, repo, ...) }

patterns.name: Config/Validate/New constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates required fields (Client not nil) via Config.Validate() and returns (lineage.Adapter, error) — consistent adapter constructor shape across the billing domain.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (lineage.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{db: config.Client}, nil }

patterns.name: Private mapLineage/mapSegment conversion helpers
patterns.description: Unexported pure functions translate Ent rows to domain types, keeping conversion centralized and isolating Ent internals from callers.
patterns.example: func mapSegment(segment *entdb.CreditRealizationLineageSegment) lineage.Segment { return lineage.Segment{ID: segment.ID, LineageID: segment.LineageID, ...} }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, and the adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self — the minimal scaffold required by entutils.TransactingRepo. No business logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never use a.db directly inside a method body — always use tx.db inside the TransactingRepo closure. Struct field db is overridden by WithTx when a caller tx is active.

key_file_guides.role: Implements all lineage.Adapter methods: bulk create lineages/segments, load active segments by realization ID or customer, close/create individual segments, and FOR UPDATE locking queries for correction and backfill paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LockCorrectionLineages and LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill call ForUpdate() — always verify GetDriverFromContext before issuing these. The standalone LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID must also use TransactingRepo, not raw db. Segments have no UpdatedAt — close-and-create is the only mutation path.

anti_patterns: Calling tx.db.X directly in a method body without entutils.TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue — falls off the caller's transaction and produces partial writes
Removing the GetDriverFromContext guard from lock methods — makes FOR UPDATE semantics unreliable outside explicit transactions
Adding business or computation logic to the adapter — persistence only; service logic belongs in lineage/service
Returning *adapter publicly instead of the lineage.Adapter interface — leaks implementation and breaks testability
Constructing adapter{db: db} inline outside New() or WithTx() — bypasses validation and breaks the TxCreator contract

decisions.decision: Every method wraps Ent calls in TransactingRepo even when no caller tx is present
decisions.rationale: Charge advancement mixes multiple reads and writes; a helper that falls off the transaction produces partial writes. TransactingRepo rebinds to the existing tx in ctx or starts a new one automatically.

decisions.decision: Lock methods assert an active transaction via GetDriverFromContext before issuing FOR UPDATE
decisions.rationale: FOR UPDATE row locks are only useful inside a transaction. An explicit guard surfaces programmer errors early rather than silently issuing no-op locks.

Other sub-packages in billing/charges need lineage segments without constructing a full adapter through DI; the standalone function accepts a raw *entdb.Client and internally wraps it in TransactingRepo for safety.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method that reads and writes lineage segments atomically

openmeter/billing/charges/lineage/servic.purpose: Business-logic service implementing lineage.Service for credit realization lineage lifecycle: creating initial lineages, persisting correction segments, and backfilling advance segments. Orchestrates multi-step segment mutation sequences within transactions, delegating all persistence to lineage.Adapter.

patterns.name: transaction.RunWithNoValue for all multi-step mutations
patterns.description: Every mutating method wraps its full sequence in transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) error { ... }) to ensure atomicity — the service holds an Adapter interface (not concrete *adapter) so it uses the transaction.Runner abstraction rather than entutils.TransactingRepo directly.
patterns.example: return transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) error { ...; return s.adapter.CreateLineages(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: Input.Validate() before any business logic
patterns.description: Every public method calls input.Validate() as its first operation and returns early on failure — before acquiring locks or performing DB reads.
patterns.example: func (s *service) CreateInitialLineages(ctx context.Context, input lineage.CreateInitialLineagesInput) error { if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }; ... }

patterns.name: Config/Validate/New constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates required fields (Adapter not nil) and returns (lineage.Service, error), consistent with service constructors across the billing domain.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (lineage.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{adapter: config.Adapter}, nil }

patterns.name: Lock-then-mutate segment pattern
patterns.description: Multi-realization mutations call a locking adapter method (LockCorrectionLineages / LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill) first to serialize concurrent writers inside the transaction, then perform CloseSegment + CreateSegment splits.
patterns.example:
lineages, err := s.adapter.LockCorrectionLineages(ctx, input.Namespace, correctionOrder)
// then iterate: CloseSegment(id, now) + CreateSegment(remainder) + CreateSegment(covered)

patterns.name: Close-then-split for partial segment amounts
patterns.description: When a correction or backfill partially covers a segment, the service closes the original and creates two new segments: remainder (same state) and covered amount (new state). This preserves an immutable audit trail.

patterns.name: clock.Now().Truncate(time.Microsecond) for timestamps
patterns.description: All segment close timestamps use clock.Now().Truncate(time.Microsecond) — never raw time.Now() — to match Postgres microsecond precision and avoid equality check failures.

key_file_guides.role: Single file implementing lineage.Service with six methods: CreateInitialLineages, LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID, LoadLineagesByCustomer, PersistCorrectionLineageSegments, BackfillAdvanceLineageSegments, CloseSegment, CreateSegment. All writes are transaction-wrapped; reads are straight passthroughs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PersistCorrectionLineageSegments iterates corrections in a deterministic order (correctionOrder slice) and verifies remaining coverage reaches zero — if a correction exceeds available coverage it returns an error. BackfillAdvanceLineageSegments only covers segments in state LineageSegmentStateAdvanceUncovered. Do not call adapter methods outside the transaction.RunWithNoValue closure.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step adapter calls — creates partial writes when any step fails mid-sequence
Calling adapter methods outside the transaction.RunWithNoValue closure — the closure's ctx carries the active tx driver; calls outside use a different connection
Skipping Input.Validate() — lock operations and bulk creates do no further validation inside the transaction
Using time.Now() directly instead of clock.Now().Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps
Adding Ent query logic directly in service.go — all DB access must go through lineage.Adapter

decisions.decision: Use transaction.RunWithNoValue at the service layer instead of entutils.TransactingRepo
decisions.rationale: The service holds a lineage.Adapter interface (not the concrete *adapter), so it uses the higher-level transaction.Runner abstraction. The adapter implements transaction.Creator, satisfying the runner contract.

decisions.decision: Lock rows before reading active segments in correction and backfill paths
decisions.rationale: Concurrent charge advancement can race to apply corrections against the same realization; locking before reading serializes writers and prevents double-application of the same correction amount.

decisions.decision: Close-and-split rather than update-in-place for segment mutation
decisions.rationale: Segments form an immutable append-only ledger of state transitions. Closing a segment and creating new ones preserves a full audit trail of how each amount was covered or corrected.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method that locks lineages and transitions segment state
